reason_of_clustering: These entities represent different expiration months for live cattle futures contracts. They are directly related as they all pertain to the pricing and trading of live cattle in the futures market, allowing investors to speculate on future prices.

Similar to live cattle futures, these entities are grouped based on their specific expiration months for feeder cattle futures contracts. They are relevant for traders focusing on feeder cattle and help in strategizing investments based on market expectations for future cattle prices.

This cluster highlights the front month contracts of both live and feeder cattle futures. Comparing these allows investors to assess market trends and potential changes in cattle pricing dynamics in the immediate upcoming month.

Entities in this cluster look at futures contracts that are set to expire in different seasons. Analyzing these can help investors understand seasonal trends affecting livestock prices, like weather patterns and feed prices.
